reasoning_quote: Baseline zero-init MLP + polynomial STLSQ establishes the first valid reference; step forcing is the dominant rollout failure, so future work should target discontinuous-input generalization and physically structured friction terms.

Degree-3 relative-coordinate SINDy with exact kinematics over-expanded the library; validation overflowed and the learned rollout diverged, so the idea is rejected despite physically cleaner position rows.

Degree-2 relative-coordinate SINDy with exact kinematics improved score and reduced complexity versus baseline; step forcing worsened, but the lower average rollout and simpler model make it the new benchmark.
Polynomial plus unary friction terms produced a dense high-gain vector field and diverged in validation; direct tanh/drag features are too ill-conditioned with the current smoother derivatives.

current_best_verify: Re-run the current best degree-2 relative-coordinate model to estimate CPU-run variance. Result score=1.419123891, rollout=1.026285235, deriv=0.357575969, worst=2.294446641, complexity=74.

threshold_045: Raise STLSQ threshold to prune weak terms while preserving relative coordinates and exact kinematics. Result score=1.395147556, rollout=1.007294894, deriv=0.352982485, worst=2.266070414, complexity=73.
